name: Atuin (Shell History Sync and Search)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Atuin SH; atuin.sh sync server is free up to 10k history items

auth.notes: Sync account for cross-machine history; local-only mode available

performance.notes: SQLite local database; sub-millisecond history search

agent_readiness.notes: Replaces shell history (Ctrl+R) with full-text search over rich history database. Stores: command, exit code, duration, directory, hostname, timestamp. Ctrl+R opens Atuin's fuzzy TUI. Self-hostable sync server or Atuin.sh free cloud. End-to-end encrypted sync. Works with bash, zsh, fish, nushell. Filter by host, directory, exit code. Statistics: `atuin stats`. SQLite database export. Rust-based. Privacy-first (E2E encryption for sync).

gotchas: Sync requires account or self-hosted server
Replaces Ctrl+R shell default — needs user acceptance
Large history databases slow first sync
best_when: Developers working across multiple machines wanting history sync
Power users wanting rich history search (by dir, exit code, duration)
Privacy-conscious users wanting E2E encrypted history sync
Modern shell setups alongside zoxide, starship, fzf
avoid_when: Environments with strict tool installation constraints
Teams with restrictive data policies (even E2E encrypted)
summary: Atuin replaces shell history (Ctrl+R) with a SQLite database storing commands, exit codes, duration, directory, and hostname. Rich TUI search. Cross-machine E2E encrypted sync via atuin.sh or self-hosted server. Bash/zsh/fish/nushell. Statistics and export. Best for developers across multiple machines wanting searchable, rich shell history. Part of the modern Rust CLI toolkit alongside fzf, ripgrep, bat, eza.
